title: silene nocturna subsp. capraria
intro: Silene nocturna subsp. capraria, commonly known as Night-flowering Catchfly or Queen's Pink, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to be effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Silene nocturna subsp. capraria are attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it beneficial for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to address issues such as colds, fever, and digestive problems.
